summary: Matched seven small functions, mostly in Kirby initialization/callback code, plus one Shrine Route ground helper. The PR replaces placeholder /// # markers with simple wrapper/body implementations and updates headers with exact prototypes for newly referenced callbacks. decomp.dev reported +304 matched bytes and 7 new 100% matches.
what_changed: Implemented ftKb_AttackDash_Coll as a collision wrapper calling ft_8008403C(gobj, fn_800F20C4).
Implemented ftKb_AttackDashAir_Phys by getting the Fighter with GET_FIGHTER(gobj), then calling ft_80085204(gobj) and ftCommon_8007D4B8(fp).
Implemented Kirby SpecialN spit/drink collision wrappers: ftKb_SpecialNSpit_Coll, ftKb_SpecialNSpit1_Coll, ftKb_SpecialAirNSpit_Coll, and ftKb_SpecialNDrink0_Coll.
Added ftKb_Init.h prototypes for previously undeclared callback targets fn_800F20C4, fn_800F6C98, fn_800F6D0C, fn_800F6D80, and fn_800F6DF4.
Implemented grShrineRoute helper fn_8020AD24(bool arg), returning true only when arg == 1 and calling Ground_801C5740(arg) in that case.
Changed grshrineroute.h prototype for fn_8020AD24 from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to bool fn_8020AD24(bool).
Included ground.h in grshrineroute.c to support the Ground_801C5740 call.
smart_moves: Used existing common fighter transition/collision helpers rather than open-coding logic; the matched Kirby functions are mostly one-call wrapper patterns around ft_8008403C or ft_80082C74 with callback function pointers.
Kept header addresses aligned with the symbol map comments when adding prototypes, preserving the file's existing address-comment convention.
Narrowed an unknown stage function signature to bool(bool), replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS only where the implementation evidence supported it.
Matched a cluster of very small functions in one PR, yielding measurable progress without broad risky refactors.
decomp_lessons: For Kirby callback wrappers, look for repeated helper patterns: grounded collision wrappers may call ft_8008403C(gobj, callback), while air/alternate collision wrappers may call ft_80082C74(gobj, callback).
When a wrapper references an adjacent unresolved fn_800Fxxxx callback, add the callback prototype in ftKb_Init.h with the address-comment style already used in the file.
Simple physics functions can require both the GObj and Fighter pointer: ftKb_AttackDashAir_Phys demonstrates GET_FIGHTER(gobj) followed by common helper calls.
Stage helpers can sometimes be matched by reducing UNK signatures to exact primitive types; fn_8020AD24 matched as bool fn_8020AD24(bool).
Only include additional headers when a new implementation needs them; grshrineroute.c added ground.h for Ground_801C5740.
naming_conventions: Kirby fighter functions use the ftKb_ prefix and action-state role suffixes such as _Phys, _Coll, and _IASA.
Still-unknown local/helper callbacks remain named fn_800Fxxxx or fn_8020xxxx rather than being guessed.
Header declarations preserve address comments such as /* 0F20C4 */ and /* 20AD24 */ before prototypes.
Fighter object parameters use Fighter_GObj* gobj; the retrieved fighter pointer is named fp.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Wrapper matching tactic: replace an unmatched marker with a direct call to a known common helper and the correct callback symbol, e.g. ft_8008403C(gobj, fn_800F20C4).
Callback-transition tactic: add prototypes for callback functions before using them as function-pointer arguments so the C type matches expected codegen.
Boolean control-flow tactic: fn_8020AD24 uses an explicit if (arg == 1) branch, calls Ground_801C5740(arg), returns 1, otherwise returns 0.
Bot report confirms 7 new 100% matches: ftKb_AttackDashAir_Phys (+52), fn_8020AD24 (+52), and five Kirby collision wrappers at +40 bytes each.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
decomp.dev bot reported matched code increased to 45.41% (+0.01%, +304 bytes) with 7 new matches.
follow_up_queries: Find other Kirby ftKb_Init collision wrappers still using /// # markers near SpecialNSpit or SpecialNDrink.
Search for ft_8008403C(gobj, fn_*) and ft_80082C74(gobj, fn_*) patterns in other fighters to match similar collision callbacks.
Audit adjacent grshrineroute functions fn_8020AD58 and fn_8020AE08 for similarly small bool or Ground_* helper bodies.
Check whether fn_800F20C4 and fn_800F6C98-family callbacks can be named or matched in future Kirby work.
